title: Copper, canyon, and the Debed River
excerpt: Alaverdi's smelter has exhaled sulphur into the gorge since 1770. Today the canyon smells of pine resin and wet stone — a landscape in slow recovery from two centuries of heavy industry.

title: The hour the khachkar catches fire
excerpt: Each October morning at Haghpat, the rising sun aligns with the gavit oculus and prints amber geometry across a ninth-century cross-stone. We timed it to the minute.

title: A barn that became a stage at 1,700 metres
excerpt: Every July, Akner village hosts chamber music inside a renovated cattle barn. The acoustics are wrong and completely right. How a small community rebuilt cultural memory in stone.

title: The botanist's pantry: a vertical honey map of the Pambak
excerpt: At 2,100 metres the meadows hum with Caucasian bees working clover and thyme. The honey is floral and faintly resinous. How the Pambak ridge became the unlikely larder of Lori.
